rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Pod worker-6875fb79d9-gjmg5 is crash-looping due to an invalid directive ('invalid_directive: true') injected into the worker-config ConfigMap via a kubectl patch at 2026-04-23T14:55:15Z. The demo-http-server application parses configuration at startup and immediately aborts (exit code 1, ~0s runtime) when it encounters an unrecognized directive. All pods launched after the bad patch fail instantly; the old ReplicaSet (worker-5ccfd4c787) remains healthy only because its pods started before the patch. Removing the invalid directive from worker-config and restarting the deployment will fully restore the rollout.
contributingFactors: ConfigMap 'worker-config' contains an unsupported directive: 'invalid_directive: true' introduced via kubectl patch at 2026-04-23T14:55:15Z
The app parses config at startup and exits immediately on invalid directives (exit code 1, ~0s runtime)
A 'kubectl rollout restart' was triggered immediately after the bad patch, creating new ReplicaSet worker-6875fb79d9 whose pods pick up the broken ConfigMap
Old ReplicaSet pods (worker-5ccfd4c787) are still healthy, masking partial service impact but the rollout is stuck

The demo-http-server application parses configuration at startup and immediately aborts (exit code 1, ~0s runtime) when it encounters an unrecognized directive. All pods launched after the bad patch fail instantly; the old ReplicaSet (worker-5ccfd4c787) remains healthy only because its pods started before the patch. Removing the invalid directive from worker-config and restarting the deployment will fully restore the rollout.
